commands.author: paulmeier

target.title: fix(matrix): honor is_direct=false on bot member event as strict-DM veto
target.body:
Fixes #85017.

## Behavior addressed

Synapse-side: `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-room.ts isStrictDirectMembership` classifies any 2-joined-member room as a strict direct room based on member count alone. The bot's own `m.room.member.is_direct: false` is read by `hasDirectMatrixMemberFlag` but never used to veto the classification.

Effect at runtime: rooms explicitly listed in `channels.matrix.groups[<roomId>]` with `requireMention: true` still get DM-routed in the inbound path (`monitor/direct.ts isDirectMessage`), so plain (non-mentioned) messages trigger the bot anyway — a documented `requireMention` boundary bypass. Workaround today: add a 3rd member to push past the 2-member heuristic.

## Real environment tested

- Host: Unraid 7.2.5, Docker bridge `towernet`
- OpenClaw: `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest` (currently 2026.5.19)
- Matrix homeserver: `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/matrix:latest` (AIO Synapse)
- Both containers on the same Docker network, Tailscale-served to `matrix.rove-goby.ts.net`
- Test room `parents` (`!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.rove-goby.ts.net`) created via `/createRoom` with `is_direct: false`, named, E2EE on, configured under `channels.matrix.groups[<roomId>] = { requireMention: true, skills: [...] }` in `openclaw.json`

## Exact steps or command run after the patch

The patch is split across three files; unit-suite invocation per ClawSweeper's acceptance-criteria block:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-room.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-management.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/direct.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/send/targets.test.ts
```

Live-environment verification of the bug-trigger preconditions (executed against the deployment via `ssh` + `curl` to Synapse client/admin APIs):

```bash
# 1) Confirm bot's member event carries the explicit signal
ssh root@<unraid-host> "curl -sk -H 'Authorization: Bearer <bot-token>' \
  'https://matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net/_matrix/client/v3/rooms/!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net/state/m.room.member/%40openclaw:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net' \
  | jq '{membership, is_direct, displayname}'"

# 2) Set is_direct: false explicitly on the bot's own membership
ssh root@<unraid-host> "curl -sk -X PUT \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <bot-token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  'https://matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net/_matrix/client/v3/rooms/<room-id>/state/m.room.member/%40openclaw:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net' \
  -d '{\"membership\":\"join\",\"displayname\":\"OpenClaw\",\"is_direct\":false}'"
```

## Evidence after fix

### Live-environment terminal output (redacted)

Setting up the explicit `is_direct: false` precondition on the bot's `m.room.member` event in the test room, observed live against the deployed Synapse:

```
--- BEFORE: bot member event is_direct field ---
{
  "membership": "join",
  "is_direct": null,
  "displayname": "OpenClaw"
}

--- PUT membership as the bot itself with is_direct: false ---
{"event_id":"$91eS_XO35q2pngx-pzPy5Xu_wANX8Lro8dvS_KPyuTs"}

--- AFTER ---
{
  "membership": "join",
  "is_direct": false,
  "displayname": "OpenClaw"
}
```

That's the exact precondition the patched `inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence` and `isDirectMessage` paths key off — `memberStateFlag === false` is the runtime signal this PR adds support for.

### Code-path verification (runtime test suite output)

Acceptance-criteria suite, runtime executed against `3218cc69`:

```
 ✓ direct-room.test.ts > inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence > honors an explicit is_direct: false on the bot's own member event as a strict-DM veto
 ✓ direct-room.test.ts > inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence > treats absent is_direct on the bot's member event as no signal (strict by member count)
 ✓ monitor/direct.test.ts > createDirectRoomTracker > treats m.direct rooms as DMs
 ✓ monitor/direct.test.ts > createDirectRoomTracker > ignores m.direct mapping when local member state explicitly sets is_direct: false
 ✓ direct-management.test.ts > promoteMatrixDirectRoomCandidate > does not classify rooms with local is_direct false as direct
 ✓ direct-management.test.ts > promoteMatrixDirectRoomCandidate > classifies a strict room as direct and repairs m.direct
   (+ 49 other tests across the four files, all green)

 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  55 passed (55)
   Duration  417ms
```

### Pre-fix live reproduction (terminal/observation logs from the deployment)

Bot responded to a plain `ping` in a 2-member room configured as a group:

| Room | Members | OpenClaw `groups[]` config | Plain `ping` (no `@openclaw`) | `@openclaw ping` |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `parents` (2 members) | `@paulmeier`, `@openclaw` | `requireMention: true`, `skills: [actual-budget, diagram-maker, news-summary]` | bot replied (BUG) | bot replied |
| `parents` (3 members, `@admin` added as workaround) | `@paulmeier`, `@openclaw`, `@admin` | same | bot ignored ✓ | bot replied ✓ |
| `hero` (2 members, no `is_direct: false`) | `@paulmeier`, `@openclaw` | no `requireMention` | bot replied | bot replied |

The only thing that fixed `requireMention` enforcement in the 2-member room was crossing the strict 2-member threshold — confirming member count is the sole gate today.

## Observed result after fix

With the three patches applied (`memberStateFlag === false` folded into `strict: false` in the helper; the inbound `m.direct` branch consults `resolveDirectMemberFlag` and short-circuits on local explicit-false; promotion-result ordering swap preserves the `local-explicit-false` diagnostic reason), the runtime test suite confirms each veto path now fires for the explicit-`is_direct: false` precondition while the legacy "absent `is_direct`, 2-member" path keeps its existing DM classification.

The behavior matrix the patch is responsible for, asserted by the suite above:

- `inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence` → `strict: false` when bot member event has `is_direct: false`
- `createDirectRoomTracker.isDirectMessage` → returns `false` for `m.direct`-mapped 2-member rooms when local member-state vetoes
- `promoteMatrixDirectRoomCandidate` → still produces `reason: "local-explicit-false"` (regression preserved via check ordering)
- Absent-`is_direct` 2-member rooms continue to classify as strict DMs (legacy preserved)

## What was not tested

- **Live runtime in a freshly-built OpenClaw image carrying this patch** — requires rebuilding `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest` (or the npm-published `@openclaw/matrix`) with the patch applied; the chunked, content-hashed output cannot be overlaid surgically onto the running container without rebuilding shared chunks. Test-suite execution against the patched source is the substitute; maintainer-side CI rebuild will produce the runtime artifact.
- **Named-room veto** (`m.room.name` present) and **`channels.matrix.groups[<roomId>]` config-based veto** — deliberately scoped out per the issue thread; tracked as a follow-up. Important Synapse finding from reproduction: `/createRoom is_direct: false` does NOT propagate the false value to member events (Synapse only adds `is_direct: true` to invites when explicitly set true; absent otherwise). So rooms created via that API don't carry the signal this PR vetoes on — they need the name/config-based vetoes to be fully covered in practice. The explicit-false case this PR handles is reachable when an admin or the bot itself rewrites its membership (as demonstrated in the terminal output above).

## Summary

Three minimal patches across the three call sites that decide DM-vs-room routing:

1. **`extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-room.ts`** — `inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence` folds `memberStateFlag === false` into `strict: false`.
2. **`extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/direct.ts`** — `isDirectMessage`'s `m.direct` branch now consults `resolveDirectMemberFlag` and short-circuits on explicit-false.
3. **`extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-management.ts`** — `promoteMatrixDirectRoomCandidate` checks `memberStateFlag === false` before generic `!strict` so the specific `reason: "local-explicit-false"` matches first; existing regression test preserved.

## Related

- #57124 — earlier partial fix for "discovered DM candidates"; this PR layers the same intent onto the three runtime classification paths.
- #85017 — the filed issue with full reproduction.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
